facts.text: Oakland became a borough in 1902, carved out of Franklin Township as part of Bergen County’s early 20th‑century municipal reshaping.
Dutch settlers arrived in 1695, establishing farms and lumber camps along the Ramapo River that shaped Oakland’s early economy.
During the Revolutionary War, Oakland’s roads supplied the Continental Army, and George Washington himself stopped by in 1777.
The 1869 arrival of the New Jersey Midland Railroad sparked modest diversification, bringing dairy production and charcoal manufacturing to the valley.
Great Oak Park preserves centuries‑old oak trees, offering a green refuge amid the borough’s suburban landscape.
The Hendrik Van Allen House stands as a well‑preserved example of early Dutch‑influenced sandstone architecture in Oakland.
